human_verification.notes: Reviewed reservation intake boundaries, hospitality/travel terminology, healthcare access prerequisites, authorization/referral/eligibility handling, PHI and minimum-necessary escalation, source research coverage, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of clinical, payer, privacy-release, rate, contract, financial, legal, and safety authority.

review_notes: All queue-level must-pass families are covered: missing prerequisite data, wrong-lane routing before execution, and attempted bypass of access, eligibility, or authorization checks.

All required healthcare domain scenario families are covered: missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependency, PHI-sensitive controlled handling, and licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation.
All required functional abilities for intake-access-and-initiation plus healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to reservation intake, readiness validation, prerequisite checks, evidence packaging, and routing before downstream booking commitments.

Authority sources cover lodging accessibility, fee disclosure for short-term lodging and live-event offers, accessible travel handling, travel reservation data terminology, eligibility, prior authorization, PHI minimum necessary handling, healthcare TPO boundaries, administrative transaction workflow, and healthcare data terminology.

Scenario set covers normal reservation intake, missing data, invalid requests, wrong-lane routing, attempted bypass of access/eligibility/authorization checks, authorization/referral dependency handling, PHI-safe handling, clinical/payer escalation, terminology normalization, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and owner-readiness conflicts.

Functionality coverage is complete in evaluation/results.json, evaluation/functionality-map.json, and this readiness evidence, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
materialization_requirements: Task metadata must identify reservation intent, requested commitment, booking channel, property or travel program, payer or sponsor, tenant source hierarchy, and downstream owner matrix.

Runtime retrieval must supply PMS/CRS/GDS, booking engine, OTA/channel manager, reservation operations, reservation CRM, PNR or locator, confirmation, itinerary, traveler or guest roster, payment/deposit, EHR/EMR, payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, privacy, and document repository hooks as applicable.
Boundary metadata must distinguish readiness validation from rate, contract, clinical, payer, privacy-release, legal, financial, safety, and reservation-execution approvals.
Refusal and orchestrator-return conditions must fire when prerequisites, authoritative source context, tenant policy, source hierarchy, or approval owner evidence is missing or conflicting.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated spec pack; tenant and jurisdiction variability is handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated specification-based operations and does not claim a retained implementation.
